sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: It looks like the green square moved to the right side of the container. The batch was stopped after 4 RIGHT actions because one of them was no_impact. Let me check the current position of the green square.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check green square position
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        print(f"Green square: boundary={n['boundary']}")
        break

# Let me check what the container looks like now
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'g' and n['pixels'] == 32:
        print(f"Container: boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The green square is now at (42, 12)-(43, 13), which is the bottom-right corner of the container. The container boundary has changed to [[38, 8], [38, 13], [41, 13], [41, 12], [42, 11], [43, 11], [43, 8]]. This is a different shape from before!
Wait, the container shape changed again. Let me compare:
- Before: [[38, 8], [38, 13], [43, 13], [43, 10], [42, 10], [41, 9], [41, 8]]
- Now: [[38, 8], [38, 13], [41, 13], [41, 12], [42, 11], [43, 11], [43, 8]]
The container shape is changing as the green square moves! This is a key insight. The container is not a fixed shape; it changes based on the green square's position.
Let me think about this. The container seems to be a "path" that the green square can move along. The shape of the container changes to accommodate the green square's position.
Actually, wait. Looking more carefully, the container boundary describes the outer perimeter of the gray area. The gray area includes the container cells AND the green square. So when the green square moves, the combined shape changes.
But the green square is 2x2 (4 pixels) and the container is 32 pixels. When the green square is inside the container, the combined shape is still 32 pixels (the green square replaces some container cells).
Actually, I think the container is a fixed shape, and the green square moves within it. The container's boundary changes because the green square is a different color, so the segmentation sees the combined shape differently.
Let me try a different approach. Let me systematically explore what happens when I click the targets with the green square at different positions. I'll start by moving the green square to the top-right corner of the container and then clicking the red target.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Green square is at (42, 12). Let me move it to the top-right corner
# The container seems to have the green square at the rightmost position
# Let me try moving up
action(['UP', 'UP', 'UP', 'UP'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
